Protein names: [Methyl coenzyme M reductase]-L-arginine C-5-methyltransferase (EC 2.1.1.379) ([Methyl-coenzyme M reductase subunit alpha]-arginine C-methyltransferase)
Organism: Methanosarcina acetivorans (strain ATCC 35395 / DSM 2834 / JCM 12185 / C2A)
Protein families: Radical SAM superfamily, MCR alpha subunit C-methyltransferase family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the radical SAM superfamily. MCR alpha subunit C-methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=L-arginyl-[methyl-coenzyme M reductase] + AH2 + 2 S-adenosyl-L-methionine = (5S)-C-methyl-L-arginyl-[methyl-coenzyme M reductase] + 5'-deoxyadenosine + L-methionine + A + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + 2 H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:66028, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:16952, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:16953, ChEBI:CHEBI:13193, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:17319, ChEBI:CHEBI:17499, ChEBI:CHEBI:29965, ChEBI:CHEBI:57844, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:166868; EC=2.1.1.379; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:29743535};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Radical SAM methyltransferase that is responsible for the C(5)-methylation of 'Arg-285' of the methyl-coenzyme M reductase (MCR) subunit alpha McrA. This post-translational methylation, despite being not essential in vivo, plays a role for the stability and structural integrity of MCR. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:29743535}.
